sections.subtitle: Recent Form - Elche
sections.text: Elche has shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins but also suffering 2 losses. Their defense has been relatively solid, conceding fewer goals in recent home games, and they’ve managed to score consistently, often taking the lead early as seen in 4 of their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form - Real Betis
sections.text: Real Betis has had an inconsistent run in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses. Their attack has been potent at times, but defensive vulnerabilities are evident, conceding multiple goals in recent away games. Both teams scoring in 9 of their last 10 matches suggests an open style of play.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Analysis
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Real Betis has dominated with wins in both encounters (3-2 and 3-0). Matches between these teams often produce over 2.5 goals (5 of 7), indicating high-scoring affairs. Betis also holds an edge in historical matchups with 3 wins in the last 5 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics and Trends
sections.text: Both teams are yet to gather points in the current season standings, sitting at 11th (Elche) and 12th (Real Betis). Trends suggest fewer than 4.5 cards in recent matches for both teams and under 10.5 corners, which aligns with a less aggressive, controlled style in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Prediction Rationale
sections.text: Given Real Betis’ historical dominance over Elche and their attacking output in head-to-heads, they are slight favorites despite inconsistent recent form. Elche’s ability to score first at home could pose a threat, but Betis’ tendency to score in most games might edge them out in a close match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Real Betis leads Elche with a score of 0-1 at Estadio Martínez Valero. The visitors took the lead through a well-taken left-footed strike by Aitor Ruibal in the 21st minute, capitalizing on a rare but effective attacking move. Elche, despite dominating possession and territory, failed to convert their pressure into goals, while Betis made the most of their limited opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Possession and territorial dominance
halftimeSections.text: Elche has controlled the game in terms of ball possession, holding a significant advantage over Real Betis. Their numerous entries into the final third and penalty area highlight a strong territorial presence. However, their inability to create clear-cut chances or register shots on target has been a glaring issue. Real Betis, on the other hand, has been far less dominant in possession but showed efficiency with a higher expected goals figure, reflecting their clinical approach when attacking.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Elche showing consistent attacking intent, maintaining pressure for the opening minutes with several spells of dominance in the opposition half. Around the midway point of the first half, Real Betis shifted the momentum, building sustained pressure that eventually led to their goal. Post-goal, Elche regained control, pushing forward with renewed vigor in multiple attacking waves, especially towards the latter stages of the half. Betis, meanwhile, retreated to a more defensive posture after scoring, with only brief moments of forward momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key individual performances
halftimeSections.text: For Real Betis, Aitor Ruibal has been the standout performer, not only scoring the decisive goal but also posing a constant threat during attacking phases. Rodrigo Riquelme’s creativity was crucial in setting up the goal, showcasing his ability to create big chances. On Elche’s side, Álvaro Rodriguez has shown promise with key passes, while Léo Pétrot and Ali Houary have contributed in both defense and attack, though without the final product in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive resilience and physical battles
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis has demonstrated a solid defensive structure despite being under pressure for much of the half. Their defenders, including Marc Bartra and Ricardo Rodríguez, have been effective with clearances to thwart Elche’s advances. Betis also holds an edge in physical duels, particularly in the air. Elche’s defense, while not heavily tested, has struggled to prevent Betis from capitalizing on limited chances, with players like Pedro Bigas and David Affengruber showing decent involvement in clearances and tackles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Elche is likely to continue their high possession game in the second half, pushing for an equalizer with increased urgency. Their focus will be on converting territorial advantage into shots on target, possibly through substitutions to inject fresh energy. Real Betis, leading by a goal, may adopt a more conservative approach, looking to protect their lead while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities. Their efficiency in front of goal could see them extend the advantage if Elche overcommits. Expect a tactical battle, with Elche’s desperation for a goal potentially leaving gaps for Betis to exploit.
